title: Entolasia stricta
taxonomy: Entolasia stricta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to Australia, and its classification further includes the genus Entolasia. The taxonomic classification of Entolasia stricta is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Clade: Commelinids, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Genus: Entolasia, Species: E. stricta. This classification helps identify and distinguish Entolasia stricta from other plant species. The classification also provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.

intro: Entolasia stricta, commonly known as the "Queensland Blue Grass," is a species of grass native to tropical Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wound healing and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Entolasia stricta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Entolasia stricta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
